title: Rule Announcement Notice: Clean Fuel Standard Updates in Washington
content: The Washington State Department of Ecology has initiated a new rulemaking process to update the Clean Fuel Standard, implementing Second Substitute House Bill 1409 which was signed into law in May 2025. This update amends Chapter 173-424 WAC and associated fee structures to aggressively decarbonize the transportation sector, the state's largest source of greenhouse gas emissions. The new rules significantly increase carbon intensity reduction targets: previously set at a 20% reduction by 2038, the standard now mandates a 45% reduction below 2017 levels by 2038, with a provision to reach 55% if specific conditions are met. Additionally, Ecology will update the carbon intensity determination tool to the WA-GREET 4.0 model. This ensures the program uses the latest scientific methodologies and aligns with other low-carbon fuel programs, driving further investment in renewable diesel, ethanol, and electric vehicle charging.
author: Analysts Account
source: Department of Ecology, State of Washnington
